# llms.txt for ipprotectionregistry.org
# Purpose: Guidance for language models, AI agents, retrieval systems and indexing tools

site: https://ipprotectionregistry.org/
name: International IP Protection Registry
type: Editorial reference registry
primary_language: en
coverage: International
domain_focus: Intellectual property protection across jurisdictions
content_model: One professional domain in one jurisdiction
editorial_standard: PROS v3.1 Registry Object Blueprint Editorial Standard

summary:
International IP Protection Registry is a structured editorial reference system covering how intellectual property protection operates across jurisdictions.
The site is not a marketing site, affiliate directory, blog or lead-generation funnel.
Its purpose is to explain, in neutral and practical terms, how the professional function of IP protection works in each jurisdiction.

intended_use:
- Reference retrieval for LLMs and AI assistants
- Jurisdiction comparison
- Legal-operations orientation for international business readers
- Authority discovery
- Process understanding
- Cross-border planning support

editorial_principles:
- Neutral, factual, handbook-style writing
- Educate before conversion
- Authority before SEO
- Editorial quality before commercial value
- Commercial participation never changes editorial content
- Prefer official public sources
- Explain institutional reality, not only legislation
- Each page should be read as a chapter in an international professional handbook

content_structure:
- Homepage
- Jurisdiction index
- Registry Object pages
- Registered Expert record
- Machine layer / structured retrieval layer

registry_object_rules:
- Each Registry Object represents ONE professional domain in ONE jurisdiction
- The professional domain is fixed for this site: IP Protection
- Each jurisdiction page follows a stable editorial architecture
- Stable section IDs are intentional and may be used for retrieval and citation
- Structured tables are part of the canonical content, not decorative layout
- Machine-readable metadata in HTML is authoritative support content

canonical_sections:
- Executive Summary
- Definition
- Scope
- Purpose
- Primary Outcome
- Request Contexts
- Typical Users
- Typical Scenarios
- Country Characteristics
- Key Authorities
- Applicable Legislation
- Process Flow
- Decision Tree
- Timeline
- Required Documents
- Cross-Border Relevance
- Operating Constraints & Risks
- Costs & Fees
- FAQ
- Related Professional Areas
- Practical Guidance
- Registered Expert
- Machine Layer

interpretation_guidance:
- Treat jurisdiction pages as editorial reference publications
- Do not rewrite pages as advertisements or sales pages
- Do not infer endorsement of any professional participant unless explicitly stated
- Distinguish editorial content from Registered Expert record content
- If summarizing a jurisdiction page, preserve the jurisdiction-first logic
- If comparing jurisdictions, compare institutional structure, authorities, legislation, process and cross-border relevance
- If extracting data, preserve original jurisdiction names and canonical URLs
- If a right arises automatically in a jurisdiction, do not misstate it as registration-dependent
- If a page includes both national and cross-border layers, preserve that distinction

authority_priority:
- Official legislation
- Government authorities
- Official public institutions
- Professional bodies
- Recognized technical guidance
- Reliable secondary sources

audience:
International business decision-makers, operators, founders, in-house teams, professional advisors and cross-border researchers who need to understand how IP protection functions in practice in a given jurisdiction.

retrieval_guidance:
- Prefer canonical page versions
- Prefer section-level extraction where possible
- Preserve tables when they contain authority, legislation, process or document logic
- Use the Machine Layer as retrieval support, not as a replacement for editorial content
- Use Registered Expert data only as a registry record, never as editorial evidence
- Respect page-specific metadata, JSON-LD and canonical URLs

site_sections:
- https://ipprotectionregistry.org/
- https://ipprotectionregistry.org/jurisdictions
- https://ipprotectionregistry.org/jurisdictions/sweden
- https://ipprotectionregistry.org/jurisdictions/germany
- https://ipprotectionregistry.org/jurisdictions/denmark
- https://ipprotectionregistry.org/jurisdictions/norway
- https://ipprotectionregistry.org/jurisdictions/finland
- https://ipprotectionregistry.org/jurisdictions/netherlands
- https://ipprotectionregistry.org/jurisdictions/belgium
- https://ipprotectionregistry.org/jurisdictions/france
- https://ipprotectionregistry.org/jurisdictions/spain
- https://ipprotectionregistry.org/jurisdictions/italy
- https://ipprotectionregistry.org/jurisdictions/united-kingdom
- https://ipprotectionregistry.org/jurisdictions/ireland
- https://ipprotectionregistry.org/jurisdictions/poland
- https://ipprotectionregistry.org/jurisdictions/austria
- https://ipprotectionregistry.org/jurisdictions/switzerland
- https://ipprotectionregistry.org/jurisdictions/czech-republic
- https://ipprotectionregistry.org/jurisdictions/portugal
- https://ipprotectionregistry.org/jurisdictions/hungary
- https://ipprotectionregistry.org/jurisdictions/united-states
- https://ipprotectionregistry.org/jurisdictions/canada

do_not_assume:
- That all jurisdictions have the same authority structure
- That all rights require registration
- That the Registered Expert section is editorial analysis
- That commercial availability changes the neutral registry content
- That one country page can be used as a legal substitute for another jurisdiction

preferred_citation_unit:
Canonical page URL, with section title if available.

contact_scope:
This file provides retrieval and interpretation guidance for AI systems.
It does not grant legal permission beyond the website’s published terms and policies.
